description:
Advances: mutagen.mutation_enumeration

Deliverable:
- `lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator.ex` (module
  `MutagenEx.MutationEnumerator`) implementing the contract in
  [mutagen.mutation_enumeration](.spec/specs/mutation_enumeration.spec.md).
- Walks the in-scope AST sub-trees from the AST cache; filters by
  `covered_lines`; consults each mutator's `validate/1`; emits
  `[%MutationSite{}]` plus a parallel `skipped` list.
- Determinism: same `{ast_cache, scope_records, covered_lines}` produces the
  same output, byte-identical, run-to-run.
- `test/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator_test.exs` covers all requirements.
- `test/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator_property_test.exs` exercises the
  determinism invariant on randomly generated input tuples.

Verification:
- Tests to pass: `mix test test/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator_test.exs`,
  `mix test test/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator_property_test.exs`.
- Covers spec-verification stubs `mutagen.mutation_enumeration.v1`, `.v2`, `.v3`.
- Behaviors to demonstrate: scenarios `mutagen.mutation_enumeration.s1` through `s6`.

Out of Scope (files):
- `lib/mutagen_ex/coverage_runner.ex` (S5)
- `lib/mutagen_ex/ast_cache.ex` (S5)
- `lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner.ex` (S5)
- `lib/mutagen_ex/mutators/**` (S4a)

Allowed touches (this stage may modify):
- `lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator.ex`
- `test/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator_test.exs`
- `test/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator_property_test.exs`

Out of Scope (intent):
- Do not read source files. All AST data comes from the (mocked-in-tests)
  AST cache per [mutagen.mutation_enumeration](.spec/specs/mutation_enumeration.spec.md) r6.
- Do not compile or run any mutation. Enumeration produces a plan; execution
  is S5.

Merge gate:
- [ ] Code compiles cleanly with `--warnings-as-errors`
- [ ] Unit + property tests pass
- [ ] Spec verification passes (`mutagen.mutation_enumeration.v1`-`.v3`)
- [ ] Scope check: diff touches only Allowed globs (mechanical)
- [ ] Auditor APPROVE comment on the ticket

IMPLEMENTED
Session: fa1552c1-29af-4847-9fbc-5065c95d3be9
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-dev-mz-mutagen-ex/fa1552c1-29af-4847-9fbc-5065c95d3be9.jsonl

Commit: fb834cc
Files touched:
- lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator.ex (new)
- test/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator_test.exs (new)
- test/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator_property_test.exs (new)
- .spec/specs/mutation_enumeration.spec.md (stub flips only — see Audit-expanded scope below)

Tests added:
- MutagenEx.MutationEnumeratorTest — 11 tests covering scenarios s1-s6 plus site shape and scope-record ordering:
  - "determinism — same inputs produce byte-identical site lists across two runs" (s1/r1)
  - "determinism holds across 10 consecutive runs" (s1/r1)
  - "nodes whose line is outside covered_lines are filtered before validate/1" (s2/r2)  [tag: filtering]
  - "fully uncovered scope produces no arith sites and a no-mutation-candidates warning" (r2 + r5)
  - "a with_swap site that validate/1 rejects appears in `skipped`, NOT in `sites`" (s3/r3)  [tag: filtering]
  - "skipped entries do NOT contribute to the sites list" (r3)
  - "enumeration walks only the in-scope module's defmodule subtree" (s4/r4)
  - "scoped module with only @callback declarations returns no sites and warns" (s5/r5)
  - "enumeration does not call File.read or Code.require_file" (s6/r6)
  - "every emitted site has the full `Site` struct fields populated"
  - "ordering: scope records are walked in input order"
- MutagenEx.MutationEnumeratorPropertyTest — 3 property tests over 100/25/50 randomly-synthesised input tuples:
  - "two consecutive enumerations on identical inputs are byte-identical" (r1)
  - "10 consecutive runs are all equal" (r1)
  - "reordering scope_records reorders sites but preserves set equality" (r1 corollary)

Verification stubs flipped:
- mutagen.mutation_enumeration.v1: execute: false → true
- mutagen.mutation_enumeration.v2: execute: false → true
- mutagen.mutation_enumeration.v3: execute: false → true

Audit-expanded scope: `.spec/specs/mutation_enumeration.spec.md` is outside this ticket's Allowed touches, but it is the spec file that owns the v1/v2/v3 verification stubs. The merge gate explicitly requires "Spec verification passes (`mutagen.mutation_enumeration.v1`-`.v3`)" which is impossible without flipping `execute: false → true`. Precedent: mutagen-wrd.1 / .3 / .4 / .5 all flipped stubs in their respective spec files outside their Allowed touches and were APPROVED. This flip mirrors that pattern and is the only edit outside Allowed touches. The orchestrator's scope note explicitly sanctioned this.

Deferred must requirements: none. All six must requirements (r1-r6) have at least one covering test with a concrete failure mode named in the test body (e.g. r2's "Line 15 produces no site" → asserted via length-and-line check on arith sites; r6's "no File.read" → enforced by passing a path that does not exist on disk, which would crash if re-read was attempted).

Summary: MutagenEx.MutationEnumerator.enumerate/4 walks the AST cache (never opens files), narrows each scope record to its module's `defmodule` body via Macro.prewalk, tries each catalog mutator in canonical order on every node, filters by covered_lines BEFORE consulting validate/1, and emits a `%{sites: [Site.t()], skipped: [skipped_entry()], warnings: [warning()]}` map. Determinism (r1) is structural — same inputs, same Macro.prewalk traversal, same mutator order, same content-addressed IDs. The property test exercises this on 100+ synthesised input tuples. mix compile --warnings-as-errors clean; full mix test suite 216 tests / 0 failures (was 202 before this ticket, +14 new tests).

VERIFY: PASS
Session: a0832dddf2959f80b
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-dev-mz-mutagen-ex/fa1552c1-29af-4847-9fbc-5065c95d3be9/subagents/agent-a0832dddf2959f80b.jsonl

Base SHA: 22a378445f77128cb2cdbd541de5dc5c31bc268d (git merge-base epic/mutagen-wrd HEAD)

Mechanical checks:
- Files touched: 4
  - lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator.ex (Allowed)
  - test/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator_test.exs (Allowed)
  - test/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator_property_test.exs (Allowed)
  - .spec/specs/mutation_enumeration.spec.md (NOT in Allowed, but sanctioned: only v1/v2/v3 stub flips, per orchestrator pre-authorization mirroring stages .1/.3/.4/.5)
- Files outside Allowed globs: 1 (`.spec/specs/mutation_enumeration.spec.md` — orchestrator-sanctioned stub flip only; diff confirmed three `execute: false` → `execute: true` edits with no other content changes)
- Files matching Out-of-Scope globs: 0 (no touches to coverage_runner.ex, ast_cache.ex, mutation_runner.ex, or mutators/**)
- mix.exs: unchanged (no new deps — Out of Scope (intent) honored)

Semantic checks:
- Spec stubs run: 3, passed: 3
  - mutagen.mutation_enumeration.v1 (`mix test test/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator_test.exs`): 11/11 pass
  - mutagen.mutation_enumeration.v2 (`mix test test/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator_property_test.exs`): 3/3 pass
  - mutagen.mutation_enumeration.v3 (`mix test test/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator_test.exs --only filtering`): 2/2 pass
- Project verification (`mix compile --warnings-as-errors && mix test`): PASS (compile clean; 216 tests, 1 doctest, 0 failures)

Requirements coverage:
- r1 (determinism, byte-identical across runs): covered by `mutation_enumerator_test.exs:39` ("same inputs … two runs") asserting `r1 == r2` and `ids1 == ids2`; reinforced by `:64` (10 consecutive runs) and the property test `mutation_enumerator_property_test.exs:42` over 100 iterations. Would fail if `enumerate/4` introduced any non-deterministic ordering.
- r2 (covered_lines filter BEFORE validate): covered by `mutation_enumerator_test.exs:89`. Asserts exactly 2 arith sites at lines [2,3] (uncovered line 4 produces NO site) AND that no arith skip entries from `lib/foo.ex` exist. Would fail if the filter were removed (length would be 3). NOTE: a flipped filter/validate ORDER is not strictly load-bearing here because Arith.validate always returns :ok — the test still passes with a flipped order. Implementation is structurally correct (cond in `try_one_mutator/5` filters before `run_mutator`). Advisory only.
- r3 (validate-reject sites go to `skipped`, not `sites`): covered by `mutation_enumerator_test.exs:144` (with_swap `:bound_var_used_before_binding`). Asserts `sites == []`, `length(skipped) == 1`, reason matches, site_id pattern matches. Reinforced by `:177` (mixed file). Would fail if skipped leaked into sites.
- r4 (multi-defmodule narrowing): covered by `mutation_enumerator_test.exs:214`. Fixture has TWO `defmodule` blocks; test scopes only Mod.A. Asserts exactly 1 arith site at line 2, AND explicitly computes Mod.B's `*` node hashes and asserts none appear in output IDs. Would fail if `find_module_body/2` walked into sibling defmodule blocks.
- r5 (empty-module warning): covered by `mutation_enumerator_test.exs:274` (Contract with only `@callback`). Asserts `sites == []`, `skipped == []`, and `{:no_mutation_candidates, Contract} in warnings`. Would fail if warning were not emitted. ADVISORY: no test asserts `warnings == []` for non-empty modules — a regression that emitted the warning indiscriminately would pass. Implementation gates correctly via before/after count comparison.
- r6 (no file I/O): covered by `mutation_enumerator_test.exs:294`. Fixture uses synthetic path `/definitely/does/not/exist/lib/synthetic.ex` with `refute File.exists?(...)`. Would fail with `File.Error` if `File.read!(site.file)` were added.

Source-claim trace: implementer comment claims 11 + 3 = 14 new tests, suite 202 → 216. Verified consistent. No deliverable self-contradiction.

Determinism by construction (r1): scope_records walked via Enum.reduce in list order; AST walked via Macro.prewalk (deterministic depth-first); mutators tried in `Mutators.all/0` canonical order; site IDs via `:erlang.phash2` of normalized AST (positional meta stripped per `mutagen.decision.content_addressed_ids`). All confirmed.

Intent-level scope concerns: none. No source-file reads (cache-only). No mutation execution. No new dependencies.

Audit-expanded scope: `.spec/specs/mutation_enumeration.spec.md` v1/v2/v3 `execute: false → true`. Authorization: orchestrator note in this verifier prompt; precedent in stages .1/.3/.4/.5 APPROVED with same pattern. Diff confirms ONLY three `execute:` lines changed — no requirement/scenario/stub-text edits.

Verdict: PASS

Notes:
- r2 test fixture would not catch a flipped filter/validate ORDER (arith validate always returns :ok). Adding a fixture combining uncovered-line + a validate-skipping mutator would tighten the regression net. Advisory — does not gate this stage.
- r5 test does not assert `warnings == []` for non-empty scopes. A regression that always emits `:no_mutation_candidates` would pass. Implementation gates correctly; advisory.
- `mix test` emits a stale-fixture warning for 4 files under `test/fixtures/spike_fixture/` not matching the test pattern. Pre-existing (mutagen-wrd.2 spike); out of this stage's scope.
comments.author: verifier

comments.text:
AUDIT: BOUNCE
Session: fa1552c1-29af-4847-9fbc-5065c95d3be9
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-dev-mz-mutagen-ex/fa1552c1-29af-4847-9fbc-5065c95d3be9.jsonl

Reasoning: Implementation is structurally correct and all 6 must-requirements have at least one covering test; mechanical scope (4 files: enumerator + 2 tests + sanctioned spec stub flip) is clean; 216/216 + 1 doctest pass; compile clean. However, r2's contract explicitly says "filtered out BEFORE `validate/1` is consulted" — the ordering clause is load-bearing must-language, and the current fixture (Arith.validate always returns :ok on valid arith ops in `mutation_enumerator_test.exs:89`) cannot fail under a flipped filter/validate order. That is a genuine coverage gap for r2 as worded, not a tightening nice-to-have. r5's "warning only on empty scope" is a softer gap (the warning's identity implies exclusivity but the must-statement doesn't say "only") — I'd accept it as advisory if r2 were the lone item, but combined the test net needs a small tightening pass.

BOUNCE → implementer:
1. Add one test under describe "scenario s2" that combines an uncovered line with a mutator whose `validate/1` returns `{:skip, _}` for the node at that line. The test should assert the result has NO entry in `skipped` for that node (i.e., filter ran first; validate was never consulted). Concrete shape: use `WithSwap` (which has a real validate path that returns `{:skip, :bound_var_used_before_binding}`) on a covered file where the offending `with` chain is on an UNCOVERED line — assert `result.skipped` does NOT contain that site_id. A regression that flips the order would route the node through validate and add a skip entry, failing the assertion.
2. Add one assertion to the existing r5 test ("scoped module with only @callback declarations") that the non-empty s1 fixture (e.g., the Sample module in determinism test or a fresh inline fixture) produces `warnings == []` or `{:no_mutation_candidates, _} not in warnings`. One-liner addition to an existing test is fine; goal is to gate against indiscriminate warning emission.

BOUNCE → verifier: The r2 ordering gap is not "advisory" — the must-statement uses the word "before" as a structural contract, so a test that doesn't distinguish before-vs-after is not load-bearing for that clause. Future verifier passes on similar ordering must-statements should bounce when the fixture can't observe the order. The r5 gap reading is fair as advisory; flagging both was the right call but the verdict should have been BOUNCE on r2.

IMPLEMENTED (iteration 2)
Session: a654a52c55782b136
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-dev-mz-mutagen-ex/fa1552c1-29af-4847-9fbc-5065c95d3be9/subagents/agent-a654a52c55782b136.jsonl

Commit: 1c4757d
Files touched:
- test/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator_test.exs (added 2 assertions; no implementation files touched)

Tests added (or tightened):
- New test under describe "scenario s2": "uncovered with-chain is filtered before WithSwap.validate is consulted (r2 ordering)". Fixture has a covered arith op on line 2 and an uncovered with-chain on line 4 whose shape (g(a) referencing `a` before binding) is exactly what `WithSwap.validate/1` rejects with `{:skip, :bound_var_used_before_binding}`. With covered_lines = [2], the contract-honoring implementation filters the with node BEFORE consulting validate, so `result.skipped` has zero with_swap entries for the file. A regression that flipped the order would route the with node through validate, add a `{:skip, :bound_var_used_before_binding}` entry, and the new `with_swap_skips == []` assertion would fail. The arith-still-fires assertion (line 2 site present) keeps the test from passing vacuously via short-circuiting.
- New exclusivity assertion appended to the existing s5 test ("scoped module with only @callback declarations returns no sites and warns"): runs the enumerator a second time on the non-empty Sample fixture and asserts `{:no_mutation_candidates, Sample} not in non_empty_result.warnings`. A regression that emits the warning indiscriminately on every scope would now fail this assertion.

Verification stubs flipped: none (already flipped in iteration 1; no spec changes this iteration).

Audit-expanded scope: none. The iteration-2 diff touches only `test/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator_test.exs`, which is in the ticket's Allowed touches.

Deferred must requirements: none.

Project verification (`mix compile --warnings-as-errors && mix test`): PASS — clean compile; 1 doctest, 217 tests, 0 failures (was 216 in iteration 1; +1 new test).

Summary: The prior auditor bounce identified two coverage gaps. Both are now load-bearing tests in the same file:
- r2's "BEFORE validate/1" ordering clause is now exercised by a fixture that combines an uncovered line with a mutator whose validate path returns a skip — a flipped order produces an observable skip entry the test catches.
- r5's warning identity (empty-scope only) is now gated by an assertion on a non-empty fixture — indiscriminate emission fails the assertion.
No implementation files were touched; no other spec stubs were flipped.

Session: a5e613485c58b8802
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-dev-mz-mutagen-ex/fa1552c1-29af-4847-9fbc-5065c95d3be9/subagents/agent-a5e613485c58b8802.jsonl

Base SHA: 22a378445f77128cb2cdbd541de5dc5c31bc268d. Iteration-2 base: fb834cc. Iteration-2 head: 1c4757d.

Mechanical checks:
- Files touched (base..HEAD): 4
  - lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator.ex (Allowed)
  - test/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator_test.exs (Allowed)

  - .spec/specs/mutation_enumeration.spec.md (orchestrator-pre-authorized Audit-expanded scope; diff confirmed three `execute: false` → `execute: true` flips only, no other content edits)
- Files touched (fb834cc..HEAD, iter 2 only): 1
  - test/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator_test.exs (Allowed) — +68 lines, 0 deletions; no implementation, no spec, no property-test edits in iter 2
- Files outside Allowed globs: 1 (spec stub flip, sanctioned)
- Files matching Out-of-Scope globs: 0 (no touches to coverage_runner.ex, ast_cache.ex, mutation_runner.ex, or mutators/**)

Semantic checks:
- Spec stubs run: 3, passed: 3
  - mutagen.mutation_enumeration.v1 (`mix test test/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator_test.exs`): 12/12 pass (was 11 in iter 1; +1 r2 ordering test)
  - mutagen.mutation_enumeration.v2 (`mix test test/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator_property_test.exs`): 3/3 pass
  - mutagen.mutation_enumeration.v3 (`mix test test/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator_test.exs --only filtering`): 3/3 pass (was 2 in iter 1; new r2 ordering test is tagged `:filtering`)
- Project verification (`mix compile --warnings-as-errors && mix test`): PASS — compile clean (exit 0); 1 doctest, 217 tests, 0 failures (was 216 in iter 1)

Requirements coverage (iter-2 focus on r2 and r5):
- r1 (determinism): covered by mutation_enumerator_test.exs:39 (two-run equality), :64 (10 consecutive runs), and property test :42 over 100 iterations. Unchanged from iter 1. Would fail if enumerate/4 introduced non-determinism.
- r2 (covered_lines filter BEFORE validate): covered by mutation_enumerator_test.exs:89 (line-filter scope, unchanged) AND the new iter-2 test at :125 ("uncovered with-chain is filtered before WithSwap.validate is consulted"). The new test is LOAD-BEARING for the ORDERING clause: fixture has covered arith on line 2 + uncovered `with {:ok, a} <- f(), {:ok, b} <- g(a) do ... end` on line 4; WithSwap.validate (lib/mutagen_ex/mutators/with_swap.ex:63) returns `{:skip, :bound_var_used_before_binding}` for that exact post-swap shape (g(a) is free over `a` which the swapped-in pattern would bind). Under the contract-honoring order (try_one_mutator's cond filters covered_lines before run_mutator at lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator.ex:217-232), no skip entry is generated; assertion `with_swap_skips == []` passes. A regression that flipped filter/validate order would route the with-node through run_mutator → validate, generate a `{:skip, :bound_var_used_before_binding}` entry, and the test would fail. Belt-and-suspenders assertion at :167 (arith site on line 2) prevents vacuous short-circuit pass.
- r3 (validate-reject → skipped, not sites): covered by mutation_enumerator_test.exs:190 + :246 (mixed file). Unchanged from iter 1. Would fail if skipped leaked into sites.
- r4 (multi-defmodule narrowing): covered by mutation_enumerator_test.exs:260. Unchanged from iter 1. Would fail if find_module_body walked into sibling defmodules.
- r5 (empty-module warning + exclusivity): covered by mutation_enumerator_test.exs:320 with the appended iter-2 exclusivity assertion at :337-357. The new assertion is LOAD-BEARING: runs the enumerator a second time on a fresh non-empty Sample fixture (two arith ops on covered lines [1,2,3,4]), asserts `{:no_mutation_candidates, Sample} not in non_empty_result.warnings`. Fixture is genuinely non-empty (enumeration produces ≥2 arith sites). Under the contract-honoring implementation (lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator.ex:178-187, gated by before/after count comparison), the warning is suppressed; assertion passes. A regression that emitted the warning indiscriminately (e.g., unconditional prepend) would inject `{:no_mutation_candidates, Sample}` and the assertion would fail.
- r6 (no file I/O): covered by mutation_enumerator_test.exs:362 (synthetic non-existent path with refute File.exists?). Unchanged from iter 1. Would fail with File.Error if File.read!(site.file) were added.

Source-claim trace: implementer iter-2 comment claims +1 test, suite 216 → 217. Verified: 12 tests in mutation_enumerator_test.exs (was 11), 3 property tests unchanged, 217 total. No deliverable self-contradiction. The new r2 test is correctly tagged `:filtering` (joins v3 stub coverage).

Intent-level scope concerns: none. No source-file reads (cache-only). No mutation execution. No new dependencies. No implementation file changes in iter 2.

Audit-expanded scope: `.spec/specs/mutation_enumeration.spec.md` v1/v2/v3 `execute: false → true` (in iter 1, unchanged in iter 2). Authorization: orchestrator pre-authorization in this verifier prompt; precedent in stages .1/.3/.4/.5 APPROVED with same pattern.

Verdict: PASS

Notes:
- Iteration 2 cleanly closes both prior audit gaps with surgical, load-bearing test additions confined to a single Allowed file. Filter/validate ordering for r2 is now observable via the WithSwap fixture; r5 exclusivity is now gated by a non-empty-fixture assertion.
- Pre-existing stale-fixture warning for 4 files under test/fixtures/spike_fixture/ remains (mutagen-wrd.2 spike); out of this stage's scope.

AUDIT: APPROVE (auto — selective audit, orchestrated, iter 2)

title: S4b: Mutation enumerator
